finding: Skill labels are multi-label and highly imbalanced.
why_it_matters_for_skill_extraction: F1-micro is useful, but common skills can dominate model performance. Per-skill review is important.

finding: Job descriptions and titles provide the model's main text signal.
why_it_matters_for_skill_extraction: Profiles without meaningful descriptions are harder to classify because the model has less evidence.

finding: The external alumni dataset is smaller but directly tests out-of-sample generalization.
why_it_matters_for_skill_extraction: This is the best check of whether the training workflow transfers to KSB alumni profiles.

finding: Explicit skill mentions are only part of the problem.
why_it_matters_for_skill_extraction: When skill names are not directly in the text, models must infer skills from job duties, titles, industries, and context.

finding: The 100-skill model space should be checked against external labels.
why_it_matters_for_skill_extraction: If many external skills are outside the modeled label space, the model cannot predict them regardless of text quality.
